name: Upper Navarrese Basque

ancestors.notes: Upper Navarrese uses the standard Basque grapheme base but exhibits systematic phonological reduction driven by Spanish contact, particularly in sibilant and lateral distinctions.

notes: Upper (High) Navarrese (Goi-Nafar). Navarrese Basque of the Pamplona basin and northern Navarre. VALIDATED and CORRECTED against Hualde, Lujanbio & Zubiri (2010), an Illustration of the IPA of Goizueta Basque, a conservative High Navarrese variety. Corrections vs. the prior draft: (1) the three-way sibilant contrast /s̻ s̺ ʃ/ (and matching affricates) is FULLY STABLE in this dialect group — the neutralisations run further west, in Gipuzkoan and Biscayan, not in Navarrese (Hualde et al. 2010: 119); the earlier claim of an apical/laminal merger to a single [s] here was unsourced and has been removed. (2) Aspiration (/h/) is absent: /h/ was lost in these varieties (Goizueta has no /h/; Hualde et al. 2010: 113 fn.1; Hualde 2018: 2). (3) Incipient yeísmo — the palatal lateral /ʎ/ delateralising to the palatal approximant [ʝ]~[j] — is documented and in progress, matching the parallel Spanish development (Hualde et al. 2010: 120). Voiced stops /b d ɡ/ are approximants [β ð ɣ] intervocalically (2010: 116). Some High Navarrese varieties (Goizueta) additionally show lexically contrastive pitch-accent (2010: 113, 123). Spanish contact is heavy in the Pamplona-basin sub-varieties, which are highly endangered.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I., Lujanbio, O. & Zubiri, J.J.
sources.title: Goizueta Basque (Illustrations of the IPA)
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 40(1)
sources.notes: Primary read source: stable three-way sibilant contrast (p.119), loss of /h/ (p.113 fn.1), yeísmo/ʎ-delateralization (p.120), pitch-accent (pp.113, 123).

sources.author: Hualde, J.I.
sources.title: Aspiration in Basque
sources.notes: Read for the absence of aspiration in Navarrese and western Basque (p.84 fn.).

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
